note: Tiered suite. easy (recipe): probe a paragraphs-specific, load-bearing fact graded on response content, no site mutation. medium (introspection): a setup script installs a known Paragraphs config to the live site, the agent inspects it via drush, graded on response content, a cleanup script restores baseline. hard (execution): a reset script clears state, the agent builds the config live, and a verify script checks real site state (exit 0 = pass).

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with the Paragraphs module, I want to let editors add paragraph components to a content type. Which field type do I add to the node, what target entity type does it reference, and which widget do I pick on the form display?
evals.expected_output: Add an Entity Reference Revisions field whose target type is Paragraph, then choose the Paragraphs (stable) widget on the host's form display.

evals.prompt: In Paragraphs, what is the relationship between a 'Paragraphs type' and a 'paragraph'? Name the entity type of each and the class of the type entity.
evals.expected_output: A Paragraphs type (config entity paragraphs_type, class Drupal\paragraphs\Entity\ParagraphsType) is the bundle for paragraph content entities (Drupal\paragraphs\Entity\Paragraph).

evals.prompt: In PHP on a Drupal 11 site, create a paragraph of type 'text' with a field value and attach it to a node's paragraphs field, then persist it. Show the code.
evals.expected_output: Paragraph::create(['type' => 'text', ...])->save(), then $node->field_content->appendItem($paragraph) and $node->save() so the paragraph revision is pinned to the host revision.

evals.prompt: Paragraphs ships two field widgets. Give the machine name of the modern default/recommended widget and the machine name of the legacy/classic one.
evals.expected_output: The default is the stable 'paragraphs' widget (ParagraphsWidget); the legacy/classic widget is 'entity_reference_paragraphs' (InlineParagraphsWidget).

evals.prompt: The main Paragraphs module has no per-paragraph-type create/edit/delete permissions. Which bundled submodule do I enable to get per-type CRUD access control?
evals.expected_output: Enable the paragraphs_type_permissions submodule for per-type create/edit/delete permissions.

evals.prompt: I want editors to author one paragraph once and reuse the same paragraph across many pages in Paragraphs. Which bundled submodule provides that?
evals.expected_output: The paragraphs_library submodule provides the reusable Paragraphs Library.

evals.prompt: In Paragraphs, which permission lets a role see unpublished paragraphs, and which config flag in paragraphs.settings must be enabled for it to take effect?
evals.expected_output: The 'view unpublished paragraphs' permission, effective only when the paragraphs.settings 'show_unpublished' boolean is TRUE.

evals.prompt: On an Entity Reference Revisions field for paragraphs, which reference-setting key restricts which paragraph types an editor may add, and which key sets the paragraph type pre-added when the form opens?
evals.expected_output: target_bundles limits the allowed paragraph types (ordered via target_bundles_drag_drop); default_paragraph_type sets the type auto-added on new host entities.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), set up Paragraphs so editors can add rich body sections to Articles. Create a paragraph type with machine name `eval_quote` that has a formatted-text field `field_quote_text`, then add a paragraphs reference field `field_eval_sections` to the Article content type that references paragraphs (unlimited cardinality). Apply everything to the live site so it really exists; don't just describe it. One-line summary.
evals.expected_output: A paragraphs_type eval_quote (with a formatted-text field_quote_text) exists AND node.article has an unlimited-cardinality field_eval_sections of type entity_reference_revisions targeting paragraph. Verified against live site state.

evals.prompt: This Drupal site (drush available) already has a paragraph type with machine name `eval_intro_pt`. Inspect the running site and tell me which fields it has. Give the field machine name(s).
evals.expected_output: The eval_intro_pt paragraph type has one added field: field_intro_body (a formatted long-text field).

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), which field on the Article content type references paragraphs, and what is its cardinality? Give the field machine name and the cardinality.
evals.expected_output: node.article's field_intro_sections (type entity_reference_revisions, target paragraph) has a cardinality of 3.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), inspect the Article field `field_intro_sections` that references paragraphs. What entity-reference target type and selection handler does it use?
evals.expected_output: field_intro_sections targets the paragraph entity type and uses the default:paragraph selection handler.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), create a Paragraphs type with machine name `eval_cta` for a call-to-action component. Give it a link field `field_cta_link` and a plain-text field `field_cta_label`. Apply everything to the live site so it really exists; don't just describe it. One-line summary.
evals.expected_output: A paragraphs_type eval_cta exists carrying a link-type field (field_cta_link) and a text-type field (field_cta_label). Verified against live site state.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), add a paragraphs reference field with machine name `field_eval_hero` to the Basic page (machine name `page`) content type. It must be an entity reference revisions field targeting paragraphs. Apply everything to the live site so it really exists; don't just describe it. One-line summary.
evals.expected_output: node.page has a field_eval_hero of type entity_reference_revisions targeting paragraph. Verified against live site state.
